howItWorks.intro: This life insurance calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Coverage = Income × Years + Debts + College Needs - Savings
Premium estimate based on $0.50 per $1,000 of coverage (term life)
howItWorks.outro: Insurance rates depend on many individual factors. Get personalized quotes from licensed providers.

faq.question: How much life insurance do I need?
faq.answer: A common rule is 10-12× your annual income. A more precise method: add income replacement needs, debts, and future expenses (college), then subtract savings and existing coverage.

faq.question: Term vs whole life insurance?
faq.answer: Term is cheaper and covers a specific period (10-30 years). Whole life is permanent with a cash value component but costs 5-15× more. Most people are best served by term life.

faq.question: How much does life insurance cost?
faq.answer: A healthy 35-year-old can get a $500,000 20-year term policy for about $25-40/month. Rates depend on age, health, smoking status, and coverage amount.

faq.question: When should I get life insurance?
faq.answer: As soon as someone depends on your income. Key triggers: marriage, buying a home, having children. The younger and healthier you are, the cheaper the premiums.

faq.question: Is the Life Insurance Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Life Insurance Calculator?
sections.body: The Life Insurance Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the insurance planning space. By entering your annual income, years to replace, total debts, you get instant results including income replacement, total coverage needed, est. monthly premium. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting income replacement right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In insurance planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to choose the right coverage with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your annual income and need to find the right income replacement. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Annual Income ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your annual income — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: years to replace, total debts, college fund needed, current savings. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your income replacement, total coverage needed, est. monthly premium. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your annual income before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both income replacement and total coverage needed — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to choose the right coverage.
If you're unsure about your current savings, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
